skill.name: mcp-builder
skill.description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools.
skill.summary: Comprehensive guide for building MCP servers in Python or TypeScript with best practices for tool design, API integration, and evaluation.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation skill containing guides for building MCP servers. Static analysis detected 498 patterns, but nearly all are FALSE POSITIVES from markdown documentation files showing code examples. The Python scripts (evaluation.py, connections.py) are legitimate evaluation tooling using official Anthropic and MCP SDKs. Minor risk from environment variable handling in evaluation scripts.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Handling in Evaluation Scripts
medium_findings.description: The evaluation.py script accepts environment variables via command-line arguments which could potentially expose secrets if logged. Variables are parsed but not validated for sensitive patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variables passed via CLI can appear in process listings and logs. The script parses them without filtering sensitive patterns like API keys or tokens.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation files contain hardcoded URLs for MCP SDK repositories and documentation sites. These are informational references, not security risks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to official MCP SDK GitHub repositories and documentation sites - these are legitimate documentation references.

low_findings.title: HTTP Client Library Usage in Examples
low_findings.description: Documentation contains examples using HTTP client libraries for API calls. These are instructional code examples, not executable code in the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: HTTP client usage is in markdown documentation showing example MCP server implementations, not in the skill's executable code.

content.user_title: Build MCP servers for Claude integration
content.value_statement: Creating MCP servers that work well with AI assistants requires careful design decisions. This guide provides proven patterns for tool design, API integration, and evaluation that ensure reliable LLM interactions.
actual_capabilities: Provides step-by-step guidance for building MCP servers in Python or TypeScript
Explains tool naming conventions and schema design for better LLM discoverability
Includes evaluation frameworks for testing MCP server effectiveness
Covers authentication, error handling, and pagination best practices
References official MCP SDK documentation and specifications
limitations: This is a documentation skill - it provides guidance but does not automatically generate code
Requires manual implementation of MCP servers following the provided patterns
Evaluation scripts require Anthropic API access and proper MCP server setup
Examples focus on FastMCP (Python) and official TypeScript SDK - other frameworks not covered

use_cases.title: API Integration Developer
use_cases.description: Developers building MCP servers to expose external APIs to AI assistants can follow the comprehensive implementation guides for their chosen language.

use_cases.title: AI Tool Designer
use_cases.description: Teams designing AI tool ecosystems can use the evaluation framework to validate that their MCP servers work effectively with LLM agents.
use_cases.target_user: AI platform engineers and tool designers

use_cases.title: Technical Lead Planning AI Integration
use_cases.description: Engineering leaders can use the best practices guide to establish standards for MCP server development across their organization.

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started with MCP
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to build an MCP server to connect Claude to [API_NAME]. Based on the mcp-builder guide, what are the first steps I should take to plan my implementation?

prompt_templates.title: Tool Schema Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design input and output schemas for an MCP tool that [TOOL_FUNCTION]. Use the patterns from the mcp-builder guide for [PYTHON_OR_TYPESCRIPT].

prompt_templates.title: Creating Evaluation Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: I've built an MCP server for [SERVICE]. Generate 10 evaluation questions following the mcp-builder evaluation guidelines to test if LLMs can effectively use these tools.

prompt_templates.title: Debugging MCP Server Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: My MCP server tool [TOOL_NAME] is returning errors when Claude uses it. Review the implementation against mcp-builder best practices and suggest improvements.

output_examples.input: Show me how to structure a Python MCP server with proper tool definitions
output: Project structure with server.py, tools/, and utils/ directories
Tool registration using @mcp.tool decorator with Pydantic schemas
Async function implementations with error handling and pagination

output_examples.input: What should a good MCP tool description include?
output: Clear summary of the tool's purpose in one sentence
Parameter descriptions explaining what each input does
Return type information and format expectations

best_practices: Use descriptive tool names with consistent prefixes (e.g., github_create_issue, github_list_repos) for better LLM discoverability
Define both inputSchema and outputSchema using Zod or Pydantic to enable structured content responses
Write actionable error messages that guide the LLM toward solutions with specific next steps
anti_patterns: Creating tools with vague names like 'process_data' that don't indicate functionality to the LLM
Returning unstructured text when structured JSON would enable better LLM reasoning
Omitting pagination support for endpoints that can return large result sets

faq.question: What is MCP and why should I build an MCP server?
faq.answer: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard for connecting AI assistants like Claude to external tools and data sources. Building an MCP server lets your API or service be directly accessible to AI agents.

faq.question: Should I use Python or TypeScript for my MCP server?
faq.answer: TypeScript is recommended for its high-quality SDK support and static typing. Python is also well-supported via FastMCP. Choose based on your team's expertise and existing infrastructure.

faq.question: How do I test if my MCP server works well with Claude?
faq.answer: Use the evaluation framework in this skill to create test questions. Run them with the evaluation.py script against your server to measure accuracy and identify issues.

faq.question: What transport should I use for my MCP server?
faq.answer: Use stdio for local servers running on the same machine. Use streamable HTTP for remote servers that need to be accessed over a network.

faq.question: How many tools should my MCP server expose?
faq.answer: Prioritize comprehensive API coverage over workflow tools. Start with the most common operations and expand based on usage patterns. More granular tools give LLMs flexibility to compose operations.

faq.question: Do I need to run the evaluation scripts to publish my MCP server?
faq.answer: No, evaluation is optional but recommended. The scripts help validate that your tools work effectively with LLMs before deployment.
